claim_text: Akhtar et al. 2017 sampled 13 powdered infant formula milk brands sold in Pakistani markets for infants aged 1 to 6 months, but did not report soy status.
quote_trace: Collection and methods sections describe 13 infant formula milk brands for infants aged 1-6 months and powdered IFM sample preparation.

claim_text: The study reported Ni from <0.001 to 50.90 mg/kg in powdered infant formula brands on an as-sold powder basis.
quote_trace: Table 4 and discussion report Ni range <0.001-50.90 mg/kg.

claim_text: Pb and Cd were reported below detection in all tested brands, but the exact Pb/Cd non-detect thresholds require unit review because Methods and Table 4 disagree.
quote_trace: Results report Pb and Cd below detection in all brands; Methods and Table 4 report different numeric non-detect thresholds.

claim_text: AFM1 was positive in 7 of 13 brands and exceeded the EU infant-formula limit of 0.025 ug/kg in 4 of 13 brands.
quote_trace: Results report 53.84% positive and 30.76% exceeding the 0.025 ug/kg EU limit.
